description: Nutrients and their assimilation, function, and interactions that affect metabolism in mammals.  Differences among species will be used to emphasize unique digestive and physiological functions and how these differences affect metabolism of nutrients. Humans will be used in some comparisons. Follows physiological progression of nutrients, starting with an overview of the digestive tract followed by water and builds on specific roles of nutrients and substrates needed to provide basic processes required for maintenance, tissue accretion, and homeostatic regulation of nutrients.

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: CHEM 341,343, (BIOCHEM 301or concurrent enrollment), or (BIOCHEM 501or concurrent enrollment)
